http.description: The top-level block for all HTTP configuration. Contains server blocks, upstream definitions, and global HTTP settings.

events.description: Configure connection processing parameters: worker_connections, multi_accept, use (epoll/kqueue).

server.description: Defines a virtual server. Each server block can listen on different ports and respond to different domain names.

location.description: Sets configuration based on request URI. Modifiers: = (exact), ~ (regex), ~* (case-insensitive regex), ^~ (prefix priority). Priority: = > ^~ > ~/~* > prefix.

upstream.description: Defines a group of servers for load balancing. Methods: round-robin (default), least_conn, ip_hash, hash, random.

map.description: Creates a new variable whose value depends on values of one or more source variables. Supports regex and default.

geo.description: Creates variables based on client IP address or CIDR ranges.

types.description: Maps file extensions to MIME types. Usually included from mime.types file.

stream.description: TCP/UDP proxy configuration block. Used for non-HTTP protocols like MySQL, PostgreSQL, DNS.

listen.description: Sets the address and port for the server. Supports IPv4, IPv6 ([::]:80), Unix sockets, ssl, http2, and default_server.

server_name.description: Sets virtual server names. Supports: exact names, wildcard (*.example.com), regex (~^www\.(.+)\.com$). Checked in that order.

root.description: Sets the root directory for requests. Full path = root + URI. Use alias to replace the location prefix instead.

alias.description: Replaces the matched location prefix with the specified path. Unlike root, does not append the URI.

index.description: Defines files used as an index when URI ends with /. Checked in order, first found is used.

autoindex.description: Enables or disables directory listing when no index file is found.

error_page.description: Defines custom error pages. Can also change response code: error_page 404 =200 /empty.gif

include.description: Include another config file or files matching a glob pattern.

default_type.description: Sets the default MIME type for responses when the type cannot be determined.

proxy_pass.description: Passes the request to a proxied server. URL can include protocol, address, URI. A trailing / on the URL replaces the location prefix.

proxy_set_header.description: Redefines request headers passed to the proxied server. Essential: Host, X-Real-IP, X-Forwarded-For, X-Forwarded-Proto.

proxy_http_version.description: Sets the HTTP protocol version for proxying. Use 1.1 for keepalive connections and WebSocket support.

proxy_redirect.description: Changes the Location and Refresh response headers from the proxied server.

proxy_buffering.description: Enables buffering of responses from the proxied server. Off = pass through immediately (useful for streaming).

proxy_cache.description: Defines a shared memory zone used for caching proxied responses.

proxy_cache_path.description: Sets the path and parameters for the proxy cache. levels defines directory hierarchy, keys_zone defines shared memory.

proxy_cache_valid.description: Sets caching time for different response codes. E.g., proxy_cache_valid 200 302 10m;

proxy_connect_timeout.description: Timeout for establishing a connection with the proxied server. Default: 60s.

proxy_read_timeout.description: Timeout for reading a response from the proxied server. Default: 60s.

proxy_send_timeout.description: Timeout for transmitting a request to the proxied server. Default: 60s.

ssl_certificate.description: Specifies the PEM-format certificate file (public key). Should include intermediate certificates (fullchain).

ssl_certificate_key.description: Specifies the PEM-format private key file.

ssl_protocols.description: Enables the specified SSL/TLS protocols. Modern: TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3.

ssl_ciphers.description: Specifies the enabled ciphers in OpenSSL format. Use openssl ciphers to list available.

ssl_prefer_server_ciphers.description: Prefer server ciphers over client ciphers. Recommended for TLSv1.2, not needed for TLSv1.3.

ssl_session_cache.description: Configure SSL session cache. shared:SSL:10m stores ~40000 sessions.

ssl_session_timeout.description: Timeout for SSL session reuse. Default: 5m.

ssl_stapling.description: Enable OCSP stapling. Sends certificate status during TLS handshake (faster verification).

ssl_dhparam.description: Specifies DH parameters file for DHE ciphers. Generate: openssl dhparam -out dhparam.pem 2048

try_files.description: Checks files in order, returns first found. Last parameter is fallback URI or error code. Essential for SPA and CMS routing.

rewrite.description: Rewrites URI using PCRE regex. Flags: last (restart processing), break (stop rewriting), redirect (302), permanent (301).

return.description: Stops processing and returns response. Codes 301/302/303/307/308 + URL for redirects. 200 + text for direct response.

set.description: Sets the value of a variable. Variables can then be used in other directives.

if.description: Conditional evaluation. Conditions: variable, =, !=, ~, ~*, !~, -f, -d, -e, -x. Use sparingly (nginx's 'if is evil').

gzip.description: Enables gzip compression of responses. Reduces transfer size but uses CPU.

gzip_types.description: MIME types to compress in addition to text/html (always compressed).

gzip_vary.description: Adds Vary: Accept-Encoding header. Important for proxy caches to store both compressed and uncompressed versions.

gzip_comp_level.description: Compression level 1-9. Higher = more compression but more CPU. 4-6 is a good balance.

gzip_min_length.description: Minimum response length to compress. Default: 20. Set higher (256-1000) to avoid compressing tiny responses.

gzip_proxied.description: Enables compression for proxied requests based on request/response headers.

limit_req_zone.description: Defines shared memory zone for rate limiting. Key: usually $binary_remote_addr. Rate: requests per second (r/s) or minute (r/m).

limit_req.description: Enables rate limiting in a location. burst allows temporary spikes. nodelay processes burst immediately.

limit_conn_zone.description: Defines shared memory zone for connection limiting.

limit_conn.description: Limits the number of concurrent connections from a single key (usually IP).

access_log.description: Sets access log path and format. Can be conditional. Use 'off' to disable.

error_log.description: Configures error logging. Levels: debug, info, notice, warn, error, crit, alert, emerg.

log_format.description: Defines a named log format. Variables: $remote_addr, $request, $status, $body_bytes_sent, etc.

add_header.description: Adds a response header. Use 'always' to add regardless of response code (including 4xx/5xx).

expires.description: Sets Expires and Cache-Control headers. Values: 30d, 1h, max, off. epoch = no-cache.

allow.description: Allow access from the specified address or network.

deny.description: Deny access from the specified address or network.

auth_basic.description: Enable HTTP Basic authentication. String is the realm name shown in the browser dialog.

auth_basic_user_file.description: Path to the htpasswd file. Generate: htpasswd -c /etc/nginx/.htpasswd username

client_max_body_size.description: Maximum allowed client request body size. Returns 413 if exceeded. Default: 1m. Use 0 to disable.

client_body_timeout.description: Timeout for reading the client request body. Default: 60s.

client_header_timeout.description: Timeout for reading the client request header. Default: 60s.

sendfile.description: Enables kernel sendfile() for serving static files. Bypasses user-space copying (zero-copy).

tcp_nopush.description: Enables TCP_CORK/TCP_NOPUSH. Sends response headers and beginning of file in one packet.

tcp_nodelay.description: Enables TCP_NODELAY for keepalive connections. Disables Nagle's algorithm.

keepalive_timeout.description: First value: server-side keepalive timeout. Second: Keep-Alive header value sent to client.

worker_processes.description: Number of worker processes. auto = number of CPU cores. Set in main context only.

worker_connections.description: Maximum simultaneous connections each worker can handle. Total connections = workers x connections.

resolver.description: Configure DNS resolvers for upstream, SSL stapling, etc. Example: 8.8.8.8 1.1.1.1 valid=300s;

fastcgi_pass.description: Sets the address of a FastCGI server (PHP-FPM). Can be TCP or Unix socket.

fastcgi_param.description: Sets a parameter passed to the FastCGI server. SCRIPT_FILENAME is essential for PHP.

fastcgi_index.description: Sets the index file name for FastCGI requests ending with /.

$host.description: Request Host header, or server_name if Host is missing. Most commonly used variable.

$uri.description: Current (possibly rewritten) URI without query string.

$request_uri.description: Original full URI with query string, as sent by the client.

$http_upgrade.description: Value of the Upgrade request header. Used for WebSocket proxying.

$proxy_add_x_forwarded_for.description: X-Forwarded-For header with $remote_addr appended. Preserves the original client chain.

$upstream_cache_status.description: Cache status: MISS, HIT, EXPIRED, BYPASS, STALE, UPDATING, REVALIDATED.

$binary_remote_addr.description: Client IP in binary form (4 bytes for IPv4). Efficient key for limit_req_zone/limit_conn_zone.
